methods.description: Process for measuring ages of stalagmite samples using U/Th methods and correcting for detrital Th.

steps.description: Sixteen 230 Th ages were measured from stalagmite using U/Th methods.
steps.searchable_summary: measure 230 Th ages from stalagmite using U/Th methods

steps.description: The samples were analyzed by a Nu Instruments Multi-Collector Inductively Coupled Mass Spectrometer (MC-ICPMS) at the Oxford University, UK.
U concentrations were measured using a bracketing standard approach. Th was measured against in-house Th standards.
steps.searchable_summary: measure U and Th concentrations using standard approaches

steps.description: Half-lives as calculated by Cheng et al. (2013) were used for calculations.
steps.searchable_summary: use Cheng et al. (2013) half-lives for calculations

steps.description: The age data was further corrected for the presence of detrital Th using bulk detrital value of + 5.38E − 06 and − 4.84E − 06.
steps.searchable_summary: correct age data for detrital Th

methods.description: Construction of an age model for the stalagmite sample using COPRA and Monte-Carlo simulations.

steps.description: The age model for the sample was constructed using COPRA, an interactive age model.
A median age model with 2000 Monte-Carlo simulations and 95% confidence interval was derived.
steps.searchable_summary: derive median age model with Monte-Carlo simulations

steps.description: The chronology of the stalagmite on either side of the middle section was modeled independently and merged to establish a composite time series.
steps.searchable_summary: merge independent models to establish composite time series

methods.description: Analysis of stable isotopes of oxygen and carbon (δ 18 O and δ 13 C) from stalagmite sub-samples.

steps.description: 861 sub-samples were extracted from Sections 1 and 3 using a New Wave Research Micromill.
Sub-samples were reacted with 100% ortho-phosphoric acid at 72 °C in a GasBench.
δ 18 O and δ 13 C values in the liberated CO 2 were measured on a Delta-V plus IRMS.
steps.searchable_summary: measure δ 18 O and δ 13 C values using IRMS

steps.description: NBS-19 was used as an international standard and a laboratory standard prepared from Makrana marble was used as an internal reference.
steps.searchable_summary: use NBS-19 and Makrana marble as standards

methods.description: Analysis of major and trace elements from stalagmite sub-samples using ICP-MS.

steps.description: Forty-seven sub-samples were milled using a dentist's drill at a resolution of 3 mm for major and trace element analyses.
Samples were milled in a clean environment and dissolved in 2% nitric acid.
steps.searchable_summary: dissolve milled samples in nitric acid

steps.description: Measured directly on an Inductively Coupled Plasma Mass Spectrometer at PRL.
For internal standardization, multiple enriched isotopes of In-Ga-209 Bi were used.
steps.searchable_summary: use In-Ga-209 Bi isotopes for standardization

steps.description: The concentrations of Mg, Sr, Ba and Mn were normalized with that of Ca.
